ddls:
CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process running on the node */,
  "host_name" TEXT /* Host name of the node */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address assigned to the node */,
  "port" INTEGER /* Transport port bound to the node */,
  "last_ping_time" TEXT /* Last time the node was successfully pinged */,
  "uptime" TEXT /* Current uptime of the node */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* Current health status of the node */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Last time the node was maintained */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  "hardware_model" TEXT /* Model of the hardware */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software running on the node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the node */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nodes_port FOREIGN KEY ("port") REFERENCES ports ("port"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nodes_host_name FOREIGN KEY ("host_name") REFERENCES hosts ("host_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nodes_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nodes_ip_address FOREIGN KEY ("ip_address") REFERENCES ips ("ip_address")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "process_name" TEXT /* Name of the process */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the process (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Time when the process was started */,
  "last_restart_time" TEXT /* Time of the last restart */,
  "memory_usage" INTEGER /* Current memory usage in MB */,
  "cpu_usage" INTEGER /* Current CPU usage in percentage */,
  "disk_usage" INTEGER /* Current disk usage in MB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hosts" (
  "host_name" TEXT /* Unique host name */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the host */,
  "owner" TEXT /* Owner of the host */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the owner */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Last time the host was maintained */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  "hardware_model" TEXT /* Model of the hardware */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software running on the host */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "ips" (
  "ip_address" TEXT /* Unique IP address */,
  "subnet" TEXT /* Subnet the IP address belongs to */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the IP (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_assigned" TEXT /* Last time the IP was assigned */,
  "next_available" TEXT /* Next time the IP will be available for reassignment */,
  "last_ping_time" TEXT /* Last time the IP was successfully pinged */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_address")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port" INTEGER /* Unique port number */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used (e.g., TCP, UDP) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the port (e.g., open, closed) */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Last time the port was used */,
  "last_scan_time" TEXT /* Last time the port was scanned for security */,
  "security_status" TEXT /* Current security status of the port */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, technician) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_time" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Last time the user changed their password */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the node */,
  "node_id" TEXT /* ID of the node being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address used for access */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the action (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "additional_info" TEXT /* Additional information about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
